title: semi_truck_fire
description: A semi-truck catches fire on the highway, requiring lane closures, traffic management, and coordination with transport authorities.

timeline.title: Fire Reported
timeline.description: Motorists report a semi-truck with smoke from the rear trailer on a busy highway.

timeline.title: Units Dispatched
timeline.description: Two fire engines and highway patrol dispatched. Unknown cargo aboard.

timeline.title: Arrival On Scene
timeline.description: Trailer rear on fire. Driver confirmed safe. Cargo manifest being sought.

timeline.title: Cargo Identified
timeline.description: Cargo is general freight — clothing and household goods. No HAZMAT.

timeline.title: Aggressive Attack
timeline.description: Two hose lines attacking fire from upwind. Third line protecting exposures.

timeline.title: Fire Extinguished
timeline.description: Main fire knocked down. Overhaul of trailer cargo required. Highway partially reopened.

timeline.title: Scene Release
timeline.description: Scene handed to highway authority. Tow and load transfer arranged.

node-1.title: Semi-Truck Fire on Highway
node-1.situation: A semi-truck's trailer is fully involved in fire on a three-lane highway. The driver is safe. Traffic is backing up. You do not yet have the cargo manifest. Approach and scene setup must be decided.
node-1.question: How do you set up on scene?

options.label: A
options.text: Position apparatus to block traffic from the fire, approach from upwind/uphill, request the cargo manifest urgently, establish a 50m perimeter, and begin fire attack only after confirming no HAZMAT cargo.

options.label: B
options.text: Begin aggressive fire attack immediately from all sides to extinguish as fast as possible.

options.label: C
options.text: Wait on the hard shoulder until the manifest is received before taking any action.

node-2.title: Traffic and Exposure Management
node-2.situation: Cargo confirmed as general freight — no HAZMAT. Fire is intense. Vehicles in adjacent lanes are slowing to watch. An overhead road sign gantry is 20m from the fire.
node-2.question: How do you manage traffic and exposures?

options.label: A
options.text: Request highway patrol to close all lanes and divert traffic via the previous exit. Deploy a dedicated line to cool the road sign gantry structure. Establish a forward command post.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep one lane open for traffic flow — closing all lanes will cause major disruption.

node-3.title: Hose Line Deployment
node-3.situation: Two lines are attacking the trailer fire. The fifth wheel and cab connector area are also showing heat damage. The cab may become involved.
node-3.question: How do you deploy your resources to protect the cab?

options.label: A
options.text: Assign a third hose line to protect the cab from fire spread by cooling the fifth wheel area and trailer front wall, while attack lines continue on the main fire body.

options.label: B
options.text: Concentrate all lines on the main fire — the cab can wait.

node-4.title: Overhaul of Cargo
node-4.situation: The main fire is knocked down. The trailer contains bales of clothing that are deeply seated and smouldering inside. The trailer walls are still hot.
node-4.question: How do you conduct overhaul?

options.label: A
options.text: Use pike poles and hand tools to open bales and expose smouldering material, applying water throughout. Use a thermal imaging camera to confirm complete extinguishment before releasing the scene.

options.label: B
options.text: The visible fire is out — overhaul is unnecessary. Release the tow truck.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The semi-truck trailer fire has been fully extinguished and overhauled. The highway has been partially reopened. A full incident report has been filed with the transport authority and the tow company has been briefed on residual heat risks.
